name: stop-hook-block-forces-tool-call
tags: stop hook, decision block, force tool call, claude code, one-shot guard, sound alert, task:aacf3ef3
body:
To make Claude actually call a tool before a turn ends (not just see context it may ignore), a Stop hook must return top-level {"decision": "block", "reason": "..."} — NOT hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext, which is passive and gets discarded once the turn is already ending. This is the only Stop-hook mechanism in Claude Code that forces continued action.

Why: additionalContext-style fields inject text into context but don't force Claude to act on it before stopping; only decision:"block" causes Claude Code to feed the reason back and give Claude another turn.

How to apply: any future "make Claude do X automatically when a turn ends" request (notifications, cleanup, logging) should use this decision:block pattern from the Stop hook, paired with a one-shot state guard (e.g. a boolean flag reset on the next UserPromptSubmit) to avoid infinite block loops — the block itself triggers a new Stop event once Claude complies.
